# Implementation Plan — In-Document Annotation Markers & Drawer↔Document Navigation

**Spec (source of truth):** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-14-revenant-annotation-markers-design.md`
**Scope tier:** Tier-2 — pure frontend (WS-C), no IPC/Rust changes.
**Plan generated by:** feature-research synthesizer from 4 research lenses (codebase, ux, architecture, review-history).

---

## ✅ Resolved decisions (human-ratified 2026-06-14)

All blocking conflicts and risk decisions are RULED. Implement against these; the per-decision detail in §2 stands as rationale.

- **D1 / D9 — tokens.css ownership:** ADOPT recommendation. `--seal-ink` / `--seal-on` land as WS-1's separate, first commit (WS-A surface). WS-C never edits `tokens.css`.
- **D2 / D10 — afterUpdate vs $effect:** ADOPT (a). Stay on `afterUpdate`; NO runes migration this round. Drop §5.1 "$effect" language.
- **D3 — preview gutter position:** ADOPT. `.pv-scroll` gets `position: relative`; seals mount as its first child.
- **D4 — popover mount:** ADOPT. Portal-mount at App root, `position: fixed`, coordinate-driven placement + flip.
- **D5 — scrollNonce:** ADOPT. Card click ALWAYS sets `activeId` + bumps `scrollNonce` (fresh-open and re-center are one path).
- **D6 — block-level wash:** ADOPT. Driven by a `[data-block-id]` attribute selector; never add a class to sanitized content.
- **D7 — block-level seals:** ADOPT. Resolve by `quoted_text` text-search; key seal/stacking off the resolved DOM block, not stored `line_start`.
- **D8 — off-by-one:** ADOPT. `data-source-line === line_start + 1`; unit-tested.
- **D11 — tab-switch reset:** ADOPT. App.svelte `$effect` clears `annotationFocus` on `$activeTab.id` change.
- **D12 — CM6 immutability:** ADOPT. Gutter + wash `StateField` in the INITIAL `EditorState.create`; drive via `StateEffect`s from a Svelte store subscription.
- **D-RISK-1 — wash on old WebKit:** RULED → **silent-skip**. Feature-detect `CSS.highlights`; wash simply doesn't paint on macOS 13 / old WebKit (seal + popover still work). Add a "wash needs macOS 14+" note to the README. NO DOM-class fallback.
- **D-RISK-2 — CM6 gutter keyboard a11y:** RULED → **mouse + keyboard shortcut now; DEFER Tab-focusable gutter markers** to a follow-up issue (tracked separately). Do NOT add `tabindex` to the CM6 gutter marker in this round; document the gap.
- **D-RISK-3 — popover z-index:** ADOPT. Host at `--z-modal` (61); clamp flip-above so the popover never renders under the toolbar's bottom edge.

**Net effect on the plan below:** §2 D-decisions are all resolved as their "Recommended" line states, except D-RISK-2 which DROPS the optional `tabindex="0"` from task T3.1 (defer to follow-up). Everything else proceeds as written.

---

## 0. Goal

Make annotations visible *inside* the document (a sumi-ink "seal" marker in the gutter of both the rendered preview and the source editor) and wire bidirectional click navigation between the `AnnotationDrawer` and the document. Clicking a drawer card scrolls its anchor into view, washes the span, and opens a shared comment popover; clicking an in-document seal highlights the matching drawer card. Light/dark parity, AA contrast, reduced-motion safety.

## 1. Approach

A new `stores/annotationFocus.ts` writable (`{ activeId, hoverId, scrollNonce }`) is the single source of truth for focus, keeping `PreviewPane`, `EditorPane`, and `AnnotationDrawer` decoupled — each reads focus and renders, none reaches into another. The preview seal layer (`AnnotationSeals.svelte`) is an absolutely-positioned Svelte overlay (never `{@html}`, so DOMPurify is bypassed and irrelevant). The wash is painted with the **CSS Custom Highlight API** (`CSS.highlights` + `::highlight()`), which paints `Range`s **without mutating the DOM** — it cannot be stripped by DOMPurify and survives re-render. The editor seal is a CM6 custom gutter (`gutter()` + `GutterMarker`); the editor wash is a `Decoration.mark` in a `StateField`. A single shared `AnnotationPopover.svelte` is **portal-mounted at the app root** (mirroring `AnnotationComposer`, `position: fixed`) and positioned from viewport coordinates.

### Architecture decisions (with the research finding that drove each)

| Decision | Rationale / driving finding |
|---|---|
| **Seal layer is a Svelte overlay, not injected HTML** | [codebase] DOMPurify is bypassed for Svelte components; only `{@html}` content is sanitized. Avoids any allowlist change. |
| **Wash via CSS Custom Highlight API (no DOM mutation)** | [architecture/review-history] `CSS.highlights` paints Ranges without touching the DOM → DOMPurify-safe by construction, survives markdown re-render. |
| **Block-level wash uses a `data-block-id` CSS rule, NOT a class add** | [ux block-wash conflict] A class added to a sanitized block is stripped on re-render. A CSS rule keyed to the existing `data-block-id` attribute (already in the sanitized output + PURIFY allowlist) is DOMPurify-safe. |
| **Popover portal-mounted at App root (`position: fixed`), placed by coordinate math** | [ux portal] `EditorPane`/`PreviewPane` containers clip overflow; `AnnotationComposer` precedent is fixed at App root. "Under the span" becomes a coordinate calc (`getBoundingClientRect()` for preview, `coordsAtPos()`/marker DOM rect for editor), and flip-above is coordinate-based. |
| **Stay on `afterUpdate` in PreviewPane (do NOT migrate to `$effect`)** | [codebase/review-history] Spec says seal impl is "not dependent on" issue #2's re-render guard, and that guard is unimplemented. `afterUpdate` is strictly correct and lower-risk; mixing runes lifecycle into a Svelte-4-API component is the riskier path. Defer migration. |
| **Seal recompute scheduled AFTER async hydration settles (not same tick)** | [review-history TRAP 2 / risk] Mermaid/hljs hydration is async; computing `offsetTop` before SVG injection mis-positions seals below diagrams. Recompute is invoked at the end of `hydrateDynamicBlocks` (after `Promise.allSettled`) and via a `ResizeObserver` on `.pv-scroll`. |
| **`.pv-scroll` gets `position: relative`** | [codebase gutter-position conflict] The absolute seal overlay needs a positioned ancestor; `.pv-scroll` is the scroll container so the overlay scrolls with content. |
| **`data-source-line` (1-based) → `line_start` (0-based) reconciled with `+1`** | [architecture off-by-one] `markdown.ts` emits `token.map[0]+1`; `Annotation.line_start` is 0-indexed. Compare `data-source-line === line_start + 1` (the existing `syncScrollToLine` pattern). |
| **Block-level annotations resolve by `quoted_text` text-search, keyed off the resolved block element** | [architecture block-level gap] Block-level annotations persist `line 0` and have no `block_id` field; seal stacking keys off the resolved DOM block, not the stored line, so seals don't pile at doc top. |
| **`annotationFocus` cleared on tab switch** | [review-history TRAP 4] Store is not tab-scoped; `{#key $activeTab.id}` destroys panes. An `$effect` in App.svelte resets `activeId = null` on tab change to prevent ghost wash painting onto the new tab. |
| **Seals iterate only `anchored` + `block_level` annotations from the start** | [codebase/ux] Same filter the drawer's `activeAnnotations` uses; detached → no seal (§6). |
| **Ink-bloom pulse `@keyframes` gets an explicit `prefers-reduced-motion` block** | [review-history TRAP 3] Token-zeroing of `--dur-*` suppresses transitions, NOT standalone `@keyframes`. Precedent: `.pv-shimmer` reduced-motion block. |
| **`--seal-ink` / `--seal-on` added to `tokens.css` as a SEPARATE first commit (WS-A surface)** | [conflict] See decision D1 below. Honors zero-overlap; WS-C consumes the tokens. |

---

## 2. Conflicts & decisions needed (HUMAN MUST RULE BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION)

The following are reproduced **verbatim** from the research lenses, each with a recommended resolution. These are blocking — the human rules on them before any PR opens.

### D1 — TOKENS.CSS OWNERSHIP CONFLICT [codebase]
> CLAUDE.md assigns tokens.css to WS-A (Foundation), but the spec (§10, surface ownership line) says 'Frontend (WS-C) + one design token (tokens.css)'. The --seal-ink/--seal-on additions belong in tokens.css. If this is a multi-workstream feature-implement run, WS-A must add the tokens or WS-C must be granted a narrow exception for these two variables. This must be resolved before any PR is opened.

**Recommended:** Treat the two tokens as a **WS-A-owned task** (WS-1 in this plan, "Tokens & shell wiring"). It is a 4-line addition committed separately, first, before WS-C work depends on it. This honors the zero-overlap rule literally and keeps `tokens.css` in WS-A hands. WS-C never touches `tokens.css`.

### D2 — AFTERUPDATE vs $EFFECT MIGRATION [codebase]
> Spec §5.1 says 'migrate afterUpdate → $effect, coordinating with issue #2's re-render guard'. PreviewPane.svelte currently uses afterUpdate (Svelte 4 legacy). Issue #2's re-render guard ... is NOT yet implemented ... The spec says the seal implementation is 'not dependent on it', but also says to coordinate with it. Implementors must decide: (a) stay on afterUpdate and add seal recompute there, or (b) do the $effect migration as part of this feature. Option (a) is lower risk and strictly correct per the 'not dependent on it' language.

**Recommended: (a) stay on `afterUpdate`.** Defer the runes migration to the v1.1 hardening work. Add the seal recompute hook inside the existing `afterUpdate` chain and at the tail of `hydrateDynamicBlocks`.

### D3 — PREVIEW GUTTER POSITION [codebase]
> Spec §5.1 says seals are in 'an absolutely-positioned overlay inside the preview scroll container'. The .pv-scroll div in PreviewPane.svelte does NOT have position:relative, and .prose also lacks it. The absolute seal overlay will escape to the nearest positioned ancestor ... Either .pv-scroll or .prose needs position:relative added, or the seals must use a different positioning strategy ... This layout detail is unspecified.

**Recommended:** Add `position: relative` to `.pv-scroll` and mount `AnnotationSeals` as the first child of `.pv-scroll` (sibling to `.prose`). Seal `top` = target block `offsetTop` relative to `.pv-scroll`.

### D4 — PORTAL MOUNTING vs. EMBEDDED RENDERING [ux]
> The spec describes AnnotationPopover.svelte as 'a single shared component used by both preview and editor' (§4) and says it is 'anchored under the annotated span.' However, EditorPane's existing add-comment-affordance uses position:absolute within the editor container (overflow:hidden), which clips anything extending beyond the pane boundary. The spec cannot simultaneously be portal-mounted at the app root AND 'anchored under the annotated span' in the pane DOM without JavaScript coordinate translation. The existing precedent (AnnotationComposer in App.svelte, position:fixed) resolves this in favor of portal mounting — but then 'anchored under the span' becomes a coordinate calculation, not a CSS anchor, and the flip-above logic must also be coordinate-based. The spec's description of placement in §4 is ambiguous on this critical point.

**Recommended: portal-mount at App root, `position: fixed`, coordinate-driven placement.** Preview anchor from `span Range.getBoundingClientRect()`; editor anchor from the gutter marker DOM node's `getBoundingClientRect()` (or `coordsAtPos`). Flip-above when `rect.bottom + popoverHeight > innerHeight`.

### D5 — SCROLLNONCE RE-CLICK BEHAVIOR vs. FOCUS CLEAR [ux]
> Section §7 says 'clicking the same card re-centers it' by bumping scrollNonce. But §7 also says 'Esc / outside-click clears activeId (popover closes, wash clears).' If a user clicks a drawer card to open annotation A (activeId = A), then clicks the document body (outside the popover) to dismiss, then clicks the same card again, the activeId was cleared — so the re-click is effectively a fresh open, not a re-center. The scrollNonce bump on 'same card re-click' only applies when activeId is already set to that annotation. The spec conflates re-click-while-open with re-click-after-dismiss — these are distinct states that need separate handling.

**Recommended:** Card click **always** sets `activeId` and **always** bumps `scrollNonce`. This unifies both states: fresh-open and re-center are the same code path (set + bump). Subscribers scroll on any `scrollNonce` change. No special-casing of "already active" required.

### D6 — BLOCK-LEVEL WASH TINT vs. DOMPURIFY CONSTRAINT [ux]
> Section §5.1 says 'for block-level anchors the wash becomes a soft full-block tint.' The preview content is sanitized HTML injected via {@html html} in PreviewPane.svelte (line 372). The CSS Custom Highlight API operates on DOM Ranges and does not mutate the DOM, making it DOMPurify-safe. However, a 'full-block tint' via a background-color on the block element WOULD require either DOM mutation (adding a class) or a separate CSS rule driven by a data attribute. If it is implemented as a class addition, DOMPurify may strip it on re-render. If it is implemented as a CSS rule targeting a data attribute already present on the block (data-block-id), it avoids DOMPurify entirely. The spec should specify which approach is used — DOM mutation approach would conflict with the DOMPurify-safe guarantee.

**Recommended:** Drive the block tint from a `[data-block-id]` attribute selector. Maintain one reactive variable (the active block id) and a scoped `:global([data-block-id="…"])` rule via a small dynamic `<style>` or a CSS variable on `.pv-scroll` plus an `:has`/attribute selector. No class is ever added to sanitized content. (Implementation note: simplest is an `activeBlockId` state → a single injected style rule in `AnnotationSeals` targeting that id; never mutate the block element.)

### D7 — BLOCK-LEVEL SEALS / SOURCE-LINE MAPPING [architecture]
> 5.1/4: block-level seals cannot use source-line mapping because block-level annotations persist line 0 and no block_id (App.svelte 158; Annotation type has no block_id field); they must fall back to quoted_text text-search.

**Recommended:** For `status === 'block_level'`, resolve the target block by searching rendered block elements for `quoted_text` (text-search), not by line. Key the seal position and stacking off the **resolved block element**, not the stored `line_start` (which is 0).

### D8 — 1-BASED vs 0-BASED OFF-BY-ONE [architecture]
> 5.1: the 1-based data-source-line vs 0-based Annotation.line_start conversion is unstated; PreviewPane.emitBlockMap currently passes the raw 1-based value, so seals would be one block off.

**Recommended:** Match using `data-source-line === line_start + 1` (or subtract 1 from the DOM attribute). Follow the existing `syncScrollToLine` reconciliation. Add a unit test asserting the off-by-one is correct.

### D9 — TOKENS.CSS SEPARATE COMMIT [architecture]
> Section 10: tokens.css is WS-A-owned per CLAUDE.md but is listed as Edited in this WS-C feature; the new seal-ink/seal-on tokens should be a separate commit to honor the zero-overlap workstream rule.

**Recommended:** Same as D1 — tokens land as WS-1's separate first commit.

### D10 — ISSUE #2 / RUNES-MODE PREREQUISITE [review-history]
> Spec §5.1 says 'migrate afterUpdate → $effect, coordinating with issue #2's re-render guard'. In reality there is no issue #2 visible in the repo's issue tracker, and the existing `isHydrating` guard is the only re-render gate. PreviewPane has not yet migrated to runes mode at all — the migration is a prerequisite the spec assumes but which is not yet complete. An implementer reading the spec would not know they need to finish this migration before the $effect coordination will work correctly.

**Recommended:** Confirm D2 — no runes migration in this feature. The `isHydrating` guard plus the post-hydration recompute hook is the coordination mechanism. Drop the §5.1 "$effect" language for this implementation.

### D11 — annotationFocus NOT TAB-SCOPED [review-history]
> Spec §7 says the annotationFocus store clears activeId on 'Esc / outside-click' but does not specify what happens on tab switch. The actual component tree uses {#key $activeTab.id} to hard-destroy panes, so the store will hold a stale activeId from the previous tab until the new pane's first render. The store reset-on-tab-switch behavior must be made explicit — it is not in the spec.

**Recommended:** Add an `$effect` in App.svelte (which already owns `$activeTab`) that resets `annotationFocus` (`activeId = null`, `hoverId = null`) whenever `$activeTab.id` changes. `scrollNonce` is left unchanged. (This is the one App.svelte touch — WS-A surface, in WS-1.)

### D12 — CM6 EXTENSION IMMUTABILITY [review-history]
> Spec §5.2 describes the CM6 gutter marker and Decoration.mark as straightforward additions, but does not address the CM6 extension-immutability constraint: extensions passed to EditorState.create are effectively frozen. Dynamic additions (like enabling/disabling the wash on activeId change) require either a StateField that reads a Compartment or a reconfigure effect. The spec says 'held in a StateField keyed off the active annotation' which implies reactive updating, but the mechanism for making the StateField respond to Svelte store changes (which are external to the CM6 update cycle) is not spelled out and is the subtlest integration point in the whole feature.

**Recommended:** Include the gutter + wash `StateField` in the **initial** `EditorState.create` extensions array. Drive reactive changes via `StateEffect`s dispatched from a Svelte store subscription: subscribe to `annotationFocus` and `annotationsStore` in `onMount`, and on change `editorView.dispatch({ effects: setActiveAnnotationEffect.of(activeId) })`. The `StateField` reads those effects in its `update`. No `Compartment`/`appendConfig` reconfigure needed because the field is present from creation.

---

### Open risks (verbatim, recommended handling)

- **[codebase] CSS Custom Highlight API on WKWebView** → feature-detect (`typeof CSS !== 'undefined' && CSS.highlights`); Vitest mocks/skips registration; real wash verified in `cargo tauri dev`. **Handling:** feature-detect helper in `annotationHighlight.ts`; smoke gate (WS-4) explicitly checks the wash renders, not just the seal.
- **[codebase] Seal position recompute after Mermaid hydration** → add a `ResizeObserver` on `.pv-scroll` + recompute at tail of `hydrateDynamicBlocks`. **Handling:** built into WS-2 task P3.
- **[codebase] CM6 gutter marker + popover interaction / mount point** → popover mounted at App root (D4). **Handling:** WS-1 mounts the shared popover host; WS-3 wires editor anchor coords.
- **[codebase] scroll-into-view coordination / no "active pane" concept** → both panes subscribe to `scrollNonce` and both scroll their own seal into view when visible (no new activePane state). **Handling:** acceptable for v1; documented.
- **[codebase] scrollNonce re-click reactivity** → subscribers watch `scrollNonce` specifically; scroll handler is idempotent and guarded against loops (scroll does not write back to the store). **Handling:** WS-2/WS-3 tasks.
- **[codebase] Range construction across text nodes** → `annotationHighlight.ts` walks the block's descendant text nodes (TreeWalker) to find `quoted_text` start/end Text nodes + offsets, spanning element boundaries. **Handling:** WS-2 P1 with sub-phrase fixtures (TRAP 6).
- **[codebase] no existing ResizeObserver** → add one in `PreviewPane`, cleaned up in `onDestroy`, not conflicting with the existing MutationObserver. **Handling:** WS-2 P3.
- **[ux] WKWebView min macOS version for Highlight API** → **HUMAN DECISION (see D-RISK-1 below).**
- **[ux] CM6 gutter marker accessibility** → **HUMAN DECISION (see D-RISK-2 below).**
- **[ux] perf: seal recompute on every render** → recompute is cheap (single querySelectorAll + offsetTop loop); gate behind a content-change check if profiling shows jank on 2000-line docs. **Handling:** WS-2 measures; debounce only if needed.
- **[ux] popover z-index vs Tauri window chrome** → **HUMAN DECISION (see D-RISK-3 below).**
- **[architecture] Highlight API fidelity unverifiable in Chromium harness** → `cargo tauri dev` smoke is a hard gate (WS-4).
- **[architecture] recompute jank on >2000-line docs** → debounce/budget if measured slow.
- **[architecture] offsetTop measured after async hydration settles** → WS-2 P3 schedules recompute post-`Promise.allSettled`.
- **[architecture] block-level seals stack off resolved block, not stored line** → D7.
- **[review-history TRAP 1–6]** → feature-detect, post-hydration recompute, reduced-motion `@keyframes` block, tab-switch reset, mandatory launch smoke, sub-phrase quoted_text fixtures. All assigned to specific tasks below.

**Three additional human decisions surfaced by the UX lens (rule before implementation):**

- **D-RISK-1 (min macOS / wash fallback):** The Highlight API is absent on macOS 13 (WebKit 614). Options: (a) document a minimum macOS 14+ and let the wash silently no-op below it (spec's stated fallback), or (b) add a DOM-mutation class fallback (works everywhere, needs DOMPurify-re-render care). **Recommended: (a)** — silent skip + a README note. The seal + popover still function; only the wash tint is absent on old WebKit. (b) reintroduces the exact DOMPurify-stripping risk we designed around.
- **D-RISK-2 (CM6 gutter keyboard a11y):** Making each gutter seal Tab-focusable in CM6 is non-trivial (CM6 focus management intercepts Tab). **Recommended:** ship mouse + a keyboard shortcut to cycle annotations (or defer keyboard-accessible gutter markers to a follow-up), and document the gap. Do not block v1 on focusable gutter markers.
- **D-RISK-3 (popover z-index vs toolbar/traffic-lights):** `--z-pop: 30` is below the toolbar; a popover flipped above near line 1–3 would clip under the toolbar. **Recommended:** popover host uses `--z-modal` (61) and bound the flip-above so it never renders above the toolbar's bottom edge (clamp `top` to `toolbarBottom + gap`).

---

## 3. File map

**New (all WS-C namespace except where noted):**
| File | Workstream | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `src/lib/stores/annotationFocus.ts` | WS-2 (shared store, see ownership note) | `writable<{ activeId, hoverId, scrollNonce }>` |
| `src/lib/annotationHighlight.ts` | WS-2 | Build `Range`(s) from block + `quoted_text`; register/refresh `CSS.highlights`; feature-detect |
| `src/lib/AnnotationSeals.svelte` | WS-2 | Preview seal overlay layer + block-tint driver |
| `src/lib/AnnotationPopover.svelte` | WS-1 | Shared comment popover (portal-mounted at App root) |
| `src/tests/annotation_highlight.test.ts` | WS-2 | Range-building unit tests (incl. sub-phrase) |
| `src/tests/annotation_focus.test.ts` | WS-2 | Store transitions |
| `src/tests/annotation_seals.test.ts` | WS-2 | Seal mapping / off-by-one / detached-suppression |
| `src/tests/annotation_popover.test.ts` | WS-1 | Placement: below-span default, flip-above |

**Edited:**
| File | Workstream | Change |
|---|---|---|
| `src/lib/styles/tokens.css` | WS-1 (WS-A surface) | Add `--seal-ink` / `--seal-on` (both themes) + reduced-motion `@keyframes` guard for bloom |
| `src/App.svelte` | WS-1 (WS-A surface) | Mount `AnnotationPopover` host at root; `$effect` resets `annotationFocus` on tab switch |
| `src/lib/PreviewPane.svelte` | WS-2 | `.pv-scroll` `position: relative`; mount seals; recompute post-hydration + ResizeObserver; wire focus store; reduced-motion-aware scroll |
| `src/lib/EditorPane.svelte` | WS-3 | CM6 gutter marker + wash `StateField` + `StateEffect` wiring; open shared popover at line coords |
| `src/lib/AnnotationDrawer.svelte` | WS-3 | Card click → set `activeId` + bump `scrollNonce`; active-card treatment |

**No changes:** `ipc.rs`, `ipc.ts`, any Rust module, `capabilities/default.json`, `markdown.ts` (block-map + PURIFY allowlist already sufficient).

---

## 4. Workstreams (zero file overlap)

> **Ownership note on `annotationFocus.ts`:** WS-1, WS-2, and WS-3 all *consume* this store, but only **WS-2 creates and owns the file**. To preserve zero-overlap, WS-2 lands `annotationFocus.ts` first (it is a 15-line writable with a frozen shape agreed below). WS-1 and WS-3 import it but never edit it. If the implementer prefers, this single small file can be lifted into a 5-minute WS-0 pre-step committed before the parallel fan-out; the parallel workstreams then never touch it.

**Frozen `annotationFocus` shape (all WS depend on this — do not change):**
```ts
export const annotationFocus = writable<{
  activeId: string | null;
  hoverId: string | null;
  scrollNonce: number;
}>({ activeId: null, hoverId: null, scrollNonce: 0 });
export function focusAnnotation(id: string): void; // set activeId=id, bump scrollNonce
export function clearFocus(): void;                 // activeId=null, hoverId=null
export function hoverAnnotation(id: string | null): void;
```

| WS | Owns (exclusive) | Theme |
|---|---|---|
| **WS-1 — Shell, tokens & shared popover** | `src/lib/styles/tokens.css`, `src/App.svelte`, `src/lib/AnnotationPopover.svelte`, `src/tests/annotation_popover.test.ts` | WS-A-surface tokens/shell + the portal-mounted popover |
| **WS-2 — Focus store + preview seals/wash** | `src/lib/stores/annotationFocus.ts`, `src/lib/annotationHighlight.ts`, `src/lib/AnnotationSeals.svelte`, `src/lib/PreviewPane.svelte`, `src/tests/annotation_highlight.test.ts`, `src/tests/annotation_focus.test.ts`, `src/tests/annotation_seals.test.ts` | Preview surface + the shared store |
| **WS-3 — Editor seals + drawer navigation** | `src/lib/EditorPane.svelte`, `src/lib/AnnotationDrawer.svelte` | Editor surface + drawer wiring |
| **WS-4 — Launch smoke & verification** | (no files; runs after merge) | Mandatory `cargo tauri dev` gate |

No file appears in two rows. WS-1/WS-3 import `annotationFocus` and `AnnotationPopover` but do not edit them.

---

## 5. Tasks

### WS-1 — Shell, tokens & shared popover

**T1.1 — Add seal tokens + reduced-motion guard** (`src/lib/styles/tokens.css`)
- Add to the light (`:root`) block: `--seal-ink: #4A453B; --seal-on: #FFFFFF;`
- Add to the dark theme block: `--seal-ink: #C2BBAA; --seal-on: #232427;`
- Add a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule that sets `.seal { animation: none; }` (the token-zeroing of `--dur-*` does NOT suppress `@keyframes` — TRAP 3). Place near the existing reduced-motion block (line ~183).
- **Verify:** `npm run build` succeeds; grep confirms both tokens present in both theme scopes.

**T1.2 — Reset focus store on tab switch** (`src/App.svelte`)
- Import `annotationFocus` + `clearFocus` from `./lib/stores/annotationFocus`.
- Add `$effect(() => { $activeTab?.id; clearFocus(); })` so `activeId`/`hoverId` clear whenever the active tab id changes (D11/TRAP 4). `scrollNonce` untouched.
- **Verify:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm run build` succeeds.

**T1.3 — Mount shared popover host at App root** (`src/App.svelte`, `src/lib/AnnotationPopover.svelte`)
- Create `AnnotationPopover.svelte`: `position: fixed`, `role="dialog"`, `z-index: var(--z-modal)` (D-RISK-3). Props: the active annotation + anchor rect `{ x, y, placement }`. Content: line chip (`L5`/`L5–L7`), status badge (`Anchored`/`Block`), quoted snippet (italic Literata, ink-left-border), comment body, two-step inline delete (replicate `AnnotationDrawer` `pendingDeleteId` pattern — no native `confirm()`).
- Placement: default below anchor; flip above when `rect.bottom + height > innerHeight`; clamp `top` to `toolbarBottom + gap` so it never renders under the toolbar/traffic-lights. Outside-click + `Esc` → `clearFocus()`. Defer the outside-click listener by one `setTimeout(0)` tick and whitelist the drawer + seals as non-dismissing (mirror `AnnotationComposer.handleOutside`).
- In `App.svelte`, render `<AnnotationPopover>` once at root, driven by `$annotationFocus.activeId` + the active annotation looked up from the annotations store; pass the anchor rect emitted by whichever pane owns the active seal (via the focus store or a lightweight rect prop set on focus).
- Follow legacy `createEventDispatcher` for `delete` to match consuming components.
- **Verify:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm run build` succeeds; `npm test` (popover tests from T1.4) green.

**T1.4 — Popover placement tests** (`src/tests/annotation_popover.test.ts`)
- Assert below-span default; flip-above when anchor near `innerHeight`; top clamp below toolbar.
- **Verify:** `npm test` → these tests pass.

### WS-2 — Focus store + preview seals/wash

**T2.1 — Create focus store** (`src/lib/stores/annotationFocus.ts`)
- Implement the frozen shape + `focusAnnotation` (set `activeId`, bump `scrollNonce`), `clearFocus`, `hoverAnnotation`. Use `writable` + factory pattern matching `annotations.ts`.
- **Verify:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.

**T2.2 — Focus store tests** (`src/tests/annotation_focus.test.ts`)
- set active; clear on Esc/clearFocus; `scrollNonce` bumps on every `focusAnnotation` incl. re-click of same id (D5).
- **Verify:** `npm test` → pass.

**T2.3 — annotationHighlight.ts + tests** (`src/lib/annotationHighlight.ts`, `src/tests/annotation_highlight.test.ts`)
- `isHighlightSupported()` → `typeof CSS !== 'undefined' && !!(CSS as any).highlights`.
- `buildRange(blockEl, quotedText)`: TreeWalker over descendant text nodes; find first occurrence at/after block start, spanning element boundaries (handles `foo **bar**`); return `Range | null`.
- `refreshHighlights(ranges, activeRange)`: register `annotation-wash` / `annotation-wash-active` in `CSS.highlights` (no-op when unsupported).
- **Tests (jsdom, mock/skip `CSS.highlights`):** quoted text found; not found → null; multiple occurrences → first; block-level (no inline range). **MUST include a sub-phrase fixture** — `quoted_text` = a short word inside a multi-word paragraph with inline `<strong>`/`<em>` (TRAP 6, e.g. "Randy" inside "Reviewed by Randy on Tuesday").
- **Verify:** `npm test` → pass; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.

**T2.4 — AnnotationSeals.svelte** (`src/lib/AnnotationSeals.svelte`)
- Iterate only `anchored` + `block_level` annotations (same filter as drawer). Suppress while annotations store `loading` (avoid stale flicker).
- Resolve target block: anchored → match `data-source-line === line_start + 1` (D8); block_level → text-search `quoted_text` across rendered blocks (D7). Skip + dev-warn if not found.
- Position each seal at the resolved block's `offsetTop` relative to `.pv-scroll`. Stack multiple seals on one block with offset `sealHeight + 2px`, keyed off the **resolved block element** (D7), not stored line.
- Render the droplet-in-a-ring seal (resting/hover/active states, ink-bloom `@keyframes` pulse with the reduced-motion guard from T1.1). Hover → `hoverAnnotation`; click → `focusAnnotation` + emit anchor rect for the popover.
- Drive the block-level full-block tint via a `[data-block-id]` attribute selector keyed to `activeBlockId` (D6) — never add a class to sanitized content.
- **Verify:** `npm run build` succeeds; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.

**T2.5 — Seal mapping tests** (`src/tests/annotation_seals.test.ts`)
- Nearest-block resolution from source line incl. the `+1` off-by-one (D8); detached annotation → **no seal** (§6/§11); block-level resolved by text-search.
- **Verify:** `npm test` → pass.

**T2.6 — Wire PreviewPane** (`src/lib/PreviewPane.svelte`)
- Add `position: relative` to `.pv-scroll` (D3); mount `<AnnotationSeals>` as first child of `.pv-scroll`.
- Stay on `afterUpdate` (D2/D10). Invoke seal recompute + `refreshHighlights` at the **tail of `hydrateDynamicBlocks` after `Promise.allSettled`** (post-Mermaid) AND add a `ResizeObserver` on `.pv-scroll` (cleaned up in `onDestroy`, not conflicting with existing MutationObserver) (TRAP 2 / risks).
- Subscribe to `annotationFocus`: on `scrollNonce` change, `scrollIntoView` the active seal/block. Use a reduced-motion-aware scroll (check `matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')` → `behavior: 'auto'` else `'smooth'`, `block: 'center'`) — do NOT reuse `syncScrollToLine`'s hardcoded smooth (§8). Paint active wash via `annotationHighlight`.
- **Verify:** `npm run build` + `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm test` green.

### WS-3 — Editor seals + drawer navigation

**T3.1 — CM6 gutter seal + wash field** (`src/lib/EditorPane.svelte`)
- Import `gutter`, `GutterMarker` from `@codemirror/view`; `Decoration`, `StateField`, `StateEffect`, `RangeSetBuilder` from `@codemirror/state` / `@codemirror/view` (all already in package.json).
- Add a `StateField` (present in the **initial** `EditorState.create` extensions array — D12) holding the active-annotation id + the annotation set, updated via a `setActiveAnnotationEffect` / `setAnnotationsEffect` `StateEffect`. The field produces the gutter markers (seal on each line with `line_start`) and the wash `Decoration.mark` over the active annotation's line/char range. Wash decoration sits **below** CM6 native selection so selection stays readable.
- In `onMount`, subscribe to `annotationsStore` + `annotationFocus`; on change `editorView.dispatch({ effects: [...] })`. Unsubscribe in `onDestroy`.
- Gutter marker `domEventHandlers` click → `focusAnnotation` + open shared popover anchored at `editorView.coordsAtPos(...)` / the marker DOM rect (D4). Add `tabindex="0"` to the marker for the keyboard path per D-RISK-2 (or defer per human ruling).
- Subscribe to `scrollNonce`: scroll the active line into view (reduced-motion-aware).
- **Verify:** `npm run build` + `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.

**T3.2 — Drawer card navigation** (`src/lib/AnnotationDrawer.svelte`)
- Import `annotationFocus` + `focusAnnotation`. Card click → `focusAnnotation(id)` (always sets `activeId` + bumps `scrollNonce`, D5).
- Card matching `$annotationFocus.activeId` gets a subtle active treatment using `--seal-ink` (e.g. left-border / soft tint) — distinct from existing hover (D7-ux / drawer-active finding). Detached section unchanged.
- **Verify:** `npm run build` + `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm test` green.

### WS-4 — Launch smoke & verification (mandatory, post-merge)

**T4.1 — `cargo tauri dev` smoke** (TRAP 5 — hard gate)
- Run `cargo tauri dev`. Open a real `.md` with **at least one anchored and one block-level** annotation.
- Verify in **both panes, light + dark**: seals render; the **wash actually paints** (not just the seal — TRAP 1); drawer card click scrolls + washes + opens popover + bloom pulse; seal click highlights the drawer card; near-bottom annotation flips the popover above (and is not clipped by the toolbar); scroll a long doc with a Mermaid block → seals below the diagram are correctly positioned after hydration; edit so an anchor moves → seal follows; tab-switch with a popover open → no ghost wash on the new tab; `prefers-reduced-motion` → no bloom, instant scroll.
- **Verify:** all of the above observed in the live binary. Build + green unit tests are NOT sufficient evidence.

---

## 6. Out of scope

- Inline editing of an annotation body from the popover (popover is read + delete only; §2 non-goal).
- Cross-surface **hover** sync (hovering a drawer card previewing the doc, and vice versa) — click-driven navigation only; hover stays within a single surface.
- Showing **detached** annotations in the document (drawer-only; §6).
- Threaded replies, multiple comments per anchor, reactions.
- The `afterUpdate → $effect` runes migration of PreviewPane (deferred to v1.1 hardening — D2/D10).
- Issue #2's re-render guard / general re-render perf refactor.
- Any IPC, Rust, `markdown.ts`, or DOMPurify allowlist change.
- Sub-block-precise seal placement (mid-large-block) — nearest-block is accepted for v1 (§12).
- Keyboard-accessible CM6 gutter markers IF the human rules D-RISK-2 toward deferral.
